title: Authentication, Authorization, RBAC, Service Accounts, Tokens, and Impersonation · Kubernetes Retrieval
summary: An on-call handoff reconstructs authentication, authorization, rbac, service accounts, tokens, and impersonation from delayed object status, challenges one retained misconception, and revises the least-privilege identity and authorization policy under changed capacity. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for least-privilege identity and authorization policy after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Trace TLS identity, authentication, user and group information, authorization, admission, validation, persistence, and audit for one request.
Separate external human users and groups, ServiceAccounts, node identities, workload identity federation, image pull identity, and bootstrap credentials.
Construct namespace-scoped Roles and RoleBindings or justified ClusterRoles and ClusterRoleBindings from verbs, resources, names, non-resource URLs, and subjects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:
